overview: Jeskai Prowess is a fast, spell-heavy deck that leverages the prowess mechanic to generate immediate combat value. By casting cheap instants and sorceries, players repeatedly buff their creatures with +1/+1 and first strike. The goal is to overwhelm opponents through efficient trading and rapid board presence before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the board with multiple first-strike creatures
Trading efficiently using prowess-triggered buffs to win combat
Casting a high density of cheap spells to generate unblockable attackers

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two cheap noncreature spells
Hands containing a prowess creature and early interaction
Hands that can play a creature and cast a spell on turn one
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fixing), at least one Prowess creature (Slickshot Show-Off, Cori-Steel Cutter), and 2+ cheap instants/sorceries (Opt, Lightning Strike, Mishra's Bauble).
Bauble is a keep with any land.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early spell density or creature threats
Hands with only high-cost cards or no interaction
Hands that cannot establish board presence by turn two
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no creatures, or only spells costing 3+ mana.

early_game: Play low-cost creatures and immediately cast instants to trigger prowess
Establish first-strike advantage to trade favorably into opposing aggro
Use Mishra's Bauble for card advantage if life total permits
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mishra's Bauble (sacrifice for 1R), cast a cheap instant/sorcery (Opt/Lightning Strike) to trigger Prowess.
Turn 2: Play second land, cast another instant/sorcery, potentially cast a Prowess creature for free if you hit two triggers.
Sequence to maximize trigger count and maintain mana flexibility.
mid_game: Maintain spell density to keep prowess creatures relevant and threatening
Use interaction to clear opposing threats while continuing to apply pressure
Go wide with multiple prowess creatures to force difficult blockers
Keep mana flexible for instant-speed responses and Prowess triggers.
Use Bauble for fixing and tempo, not just mana.
Maintain hand size by cycling Opt/Brainstorm.
Treat the exiled top card as a resource for future turns.
Commit threats when you have enough Prowess triggers to make them unblockable or too large to answer.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or hard counters.
late_game: Transition to larger threats while continuing to cast cheap spells for buffs
Stabilize the board by trading efficiently and leveraging first strike
Push for lethal with buffed attackers or value out the opponent with card draw
Cori-Steel Cutter with multiple Prowess triggers (haste, large size), Slickshot Show-Off with flying, or other Prowess creatures cast for free.
Win by overwhelming the board with unblockable or unanswerable threats.
Go all-in when you have 3+ Prowess triggers in a turn, the opponent has no sweepers/counters, or you can close the game before they can stabilize.
Also commit when you can cast a Prowess creature for free and it's too large to block.

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Cast early to establish board presence; buff with every noncreature spell to create efficient first-strike attackers.

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.notes: Provides redundancy and board pressure; use alongside other creatures to split damage and trigger prowess repeatedly.

key_cards.name: Mishra's Bauble
key_cards.notes: Tap and pay life to draw cards, fueling spell density; manage life total carefully to avoid dying to the cost.

sequencing_tips: Cast noncreature spells before combat to maximize prowess buffs for the turn
Prioritize first-strike triggers to win combat trades before opponents can retaliate
Sequence cheap spells to maintain tempo and keep the board active
Save interaction for when it can clear a threat and still allow a prowess trigger
Common mistake: Overcommitting to Prowess triggers without considering the opponent's interaction.
Always leave up mana for counterspells or removal if needed.
Bauble is a tempo tool, not just mana fixing.
Prowess triggers are cumulative, so sequence spells to maximize free casts.
Don't forget that exiling the top card of your library can be a resource for future turns.

matchups.advice: Race efficiently by leveraging first strike to trade up into larger creatures.
Apply relentless pressure before they can stabilize with removal and counterspells.
Go wide and force them to use removal on multiple targets while maintaining spell density.
Use Bauble and cheap instants to trigger Prowess before casting threats. Cast Prowess creatures for free via triggers. Use instant-speed threats to bypass sorcery-speed counters.
Pressure early with Bauble and cheap spells. Use removal to deal with ramp creatures. Don't let them stabilize; race them with Prowess threats.
Win by having better sequencing, more Prowess creatures, and better disruption (discard, artifact hate). Prioritize clearing their Prowess creatures and out-triggering them.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), counterspells (Force of Will, Spell Pierce), and graveyard strategies.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or hold up mana for removal.
Track how many triggers they've left open.
